symbols: document and desk — the instruments of legal authority used to impose colonial power
portrait on the wall — the distant sovereign whose authority legitimizes the proceedings
seated indigenous figures — the subjected position of those whose land and truth are being claimed
standing officials — the dominant posture of colonial authority
description: An interior scene depicting a formal meeting between colonial officials and Indigenous peoples. Several men in 19th-century Western attire stand around a desk where a central figure presides over documents. Indigenous figures sit at the table, one gesturing as if in protest or negotiation. A framed portrait of a woman in royal attire hangs prominently on the wall behind them. A uniformed officer stands at the far left. The composition conveys an imbalance of power, with the colonial figures standing and dominating the space while Indigenous representatives are seated.
